observations.recommendation:
Foregoing considered, we reiterated our audit recommendations in prior years that
the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Members of the Inventory Committee to fully account for all the PPEs of the
Municipality and conduct investigation/disposition of those properties reported in
lumpsum amounts;
b. Inventory Committee to prepare the RPCPPE properly using the format and
contents prescribed under Annex 49, Chapter II of the NGAS Manual for LGUs,
Volume II and ensure that the same be submitted to the Auditor concerned on or
before January 31 of each year. Failure to do so shall automatically cause the
suspension of payment of salaries of the officials concerned until they shall have
complied with the requirements of the Commission;
c. ICO-Municipal General Services Officer to prepare and submit to the Audit Team
the Physical Inventory Plan (PIP), setting forth the target activities in the validation
and implementation of the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts balances;
d. Municipal Accountant to account for and recognize in the books of the
Municipality all the procured goods, and constructed and completed infrastructure
assets that are still not transferred and/or unrecorded to appropriate PPE account
classifications. Submit to the Audit Team the Journal Entry Vouchers drawn relative
thereto;
e. Municipal Accountant and ICO-MGSO to prepare and maintain updated records
specifically of the lacking and outdated PPELCs and Property Cards, respectively;
and
f. concerned offices to strictly comply with the provisions of Section 375 RA No.
7160, Sections 111 and 122 of PD No. 1445, and COA Circular No. 2020-006.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to prepare the Schedule of Unreleased Checks annually and
submit it to the Municipal Accountant in time for the closing of the books;
b. Municipal Accountant to draw the adjusting entry to restore the Cash in the Bank
and appropriate liability accounts equivalent to the amount of unreleased checks
at the end of the year; and
c. Municipal Accountant to cause the preparation and submission of the monthly
Bank Reconciliation Statement for all depository accounts of the Municipality
upon receipt of the Bank Statements.

In reiteration, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to strategize ways and exert all efforts to collect the
unsettled cash advances from persons liable, and require the concerned officials
and employees to immediately settle their outstanding cash advances;
b. Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to refrain from granting
subsequent cash advances to concerned officials and employees especially at
the close of the year unless the previous cash advances given to them had been
42
liquidated. Ensure that all cash advances are fully settled at the end of the year;
c. Strict compliance with the rules and regulations on the granting and liquidation of
cash advances pursuant to Presidential Decree No. 1445 and COA Circular No.
97-002.

We reiterated our recommendation in previous year that the Municipal Mayor require
strict implementation of the limit on the grant of cash advances to the concerned
accountable officers to ensure that their actual cash custody is within the maximum
amount covered by the bond. For increased accountability, additional bond shall be
applied beforehand in accordance with Section 305(f) of Presidential Decree No. 1445
and Section 7.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require each of the Accountable Officers
performing disbursing functions to prepare and keep updated cashbook. Make sure
that all related cash transactions are properly and timely recorded in the books.

Also, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Accountable Officers
and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records regularly and ensure that the
arrived book balances correspond to the actual cash in the possession of the AO at
all times. Henceforth, strictly enforce the rules and regulations embodied under
COA Circular No. 97-002.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to:
a. verify the cause of the discrepancies totaling P5,796,386.94 and submit the
correct details of Other Receipts and Other Expenses accounts, as well as the Net
Cash Flows from Operating Activities and its Reconciliation to Surplus;
b. for substantial and transparent financial reports, disclose the reconciliation of
Actual Amounts on a Comparable Basis and Actual Amounts in the Financial
Statements and provide adequate information for accounts with materials
balances and significant changes in the financial statements; and
c. fully observe the disclosure requirements of IPSAS and the use of the proforma
Notes to Financial Statements as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2016-004.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. draw journal entries to establish the correct balances of the Net Asset/Equity and
affected liability and asset accounts;
b. observe the proper journal entries in recording trust receipts and disbursements
under the Trust Fund and in closing the same upon fulfillment of the purpose or
condition for which these were granted/transferred and prepare the Statement of
Financial Performance of the Trust Fund. Refrain from making shortcut entries for
proper monitoring of the balances of the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Health Officer, as the Head of the Requesting Unit, to prepare the
Obligation Request (OBr) and forward this, together with the appropriate
supporting documents, to the Municipal Budget Officer.
b. Municipal Health Officer to submit to the Municipal Accountant the
consumption/distribution and inventory reports for stocks such as but not limited
to COVID-19 vaccines, drugs and medical supplies that are still in the possession
of the RHU as bases in determining the amount consumed/distributed to end-
users and the stock available, particularly at year-end;
c. Municipal Accountant to recognize first in the appropriate Inventory and
Accounts Payable accounts the purchased drugs and medical supplies before
recording the corresponding expenses. He must be cautious in recording the
financial transactions of the Municipality in its books of accounts to ensure a
reliable and fairly presented accounts balances. Also, he has to make certain that
each transaction is substantiated with complete supporting documents before
their recognition in the books; and
d. Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that lawful expenditures are completely
recorded as obligations in the year they are incurred.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to draw a
Journal Entry Voucher to rectify/recognize the appropriate expense account affected
in prior year. Henceforth, strictly adhere to the rules and regulations on the proper
recording and reporting of the financial transactions in the books.

Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to:
a. Immediately cause the submission of the liquidation reports totaling P1,772,883.71
and the lacking supporting documents of the DVs and LRs;
b. Refrain from processing payments and settlements with inadequate documents
and ensure that financial transactions are substantiated before recording them in
the books of the Municipality; and
c. Strictly comply with the prescribed laws, rules and regulations in the timely
submission of Disbursement Vouchers and Liquidations Reports, including
complete supporting documents.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the balances of the RPT and SET Receivables and
their reciprocal accounts with the total Tax Delinquency reported, particularly at the
end of the year. Thereafter, makes the necessary adjustments to their respective
records.

Therefore, we reiterated our previous audit recommendations that the Municipal
Mayor direct the concerned Municipal officials to prioritize the implementation of the
project for the provision of a suitable and adequate size storage facility with installed
racks that can accommodate all the financial reports and other related documents of
the Municipality and its 42 component barangays considering the ten-year
preservation period of the accounts. In doing so, strictly adhere to the applicable
government accounting, procurement and auditing rules and regulations.

Foregoing considered, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal General Services Officer and Municipal Health Officer to submit an
explanation and/or supporting documents on the noted deficiencies in the
Inspection and Acceptance Report and Property Transfer Reports;
b. Municipal General Services Officer, Municipal Planning and Development Officer
and BAC Secretariat to review and update the Annual and Supplemental
Procurement Plan (APP/SPP) before submitting for approval to the Head of the
Procuring Entity (HoPE) to ensure that accurate information will be made
available to the end-users;
c. Municipal Accountant to stop the practice of recording the procurement of
supplies and materials as outright expenses, instead such shall be first coursed
through as Inventory and then recognized as an expense upon consumption,
issuance, or distribution to the implementing offices or end-users; and
d. BAC Secretariat to comply with the posting requirement in the GPPB Online
Portal all the necessary information related to the procurement of COVID-19
vaccines, and ancillary supplies and services or other goods and services
necessary for the storage, transport, deployment, and administration of COVID-19
vaccines under the Republic Act (RA) No. 11525 or the COVID-19 Vaccination
Program Act of 2021.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the members of the Municipal
Planning and Development Council strictly adhere to the provisions of DBM-DOF-
DILG JMC No. 1 in the appropriation and utilization of the 20 percent Development
Funds whereby only those development projects are to be given allocation from such
fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. BAC Secretariat, Municipal General Services Officer, and the Municipal
Accountant to submit the lacking supporting documents of the DVs to the
Audit Team and provide justification on other noted inconsistencies and
deficiencies;
b. Municipal Accountant and BAC Secretariat to ensure that all subsequent
procurement projects are supported with complete documentation and
properly accomplished and signed documents in compliance with the existing
laws, rules and regulations; and
c. BAC Secretariat to ensure compliance with the posting requirements for
Request for Quotation, Notice of Award and Purchase Order under the
alternative mode of procurement.

Foregoing considered, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the
members of the Municipal Planning and Development Council (MPDC) to:
a. assess the viability of the identified PPAs in the planning stage to ensure that
only those projects which are procurement and implementation ready will be
given budget allocation; and
b. ensure the optimal and timely implementation of the projects to be funded by the
20% Development Fund to address the development needs of the residents and
to reap the desirable socio-economic benefits at the earliest pursuant to DBM-
DOF- DILG JMC No. 2020-01.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction
and Management Council to:
a. ensure that the identified priority PPAs in the LDRRMF Investment Plan be
implemented optimally so that local constituents and the community as a whole
would benefit therefrom at the earliest; and
b. submit the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of the LDRRM Funds to
COA on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal GSO to: a. Number each type of supplies and maintain Stock Ledger Cards per stock number; b. Forward copy of the Supplies Ledger Cards prepared by the Acceptance and Inspection Report (AIR) to the Municipal Accountant for preparation of the DV and recording of deliveries in the appropriate accounts; c. Utilize and consolidate weekly the Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) for supplies and materials and submit same to the Municipal Accountant with original copies of RIS; and d. Use the prescribed format in reporting the physical count of inventories and submit same to the Office of the Auditor not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year in coordination with the Inventory Committee. Municipal Accountant to maintain Supplies Ledger Cards per stock number and use the appropriate inventory account in recording the purchases and corresponding expense account upon receipt of SSMI.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by the Municipal GSO and Municipal Accountant at the time of the audit. The Municipal GSO assigned personnel to assist with the inventory but the ledger cards were not yet prepared. The Municipal Accountant planned to maintain the supplies ledger cards and review the records.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower to perform the required tasks and to maintain the necessary ledgers. The concerned officials did not give priority to the implementation of the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to closely monitor and institute corrective measures on the findings of COA-TAS and immediately inform the audit team of the courses of actions taken by the contractor. Otherwise demand payment from the contractor for the amount of ₱491,563.93.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer coordinated with the contractor to address the technical findings and provided corrective measures where possible; the contractor took actions to remedy certain deficiencies.
observations.reason: The contractor had to make corrections; however, as of report completion the corrective works and final verification by COA-TAS were not fully completed and the demand for payment had not been finalized.

observations.recommendation:
Foregoing observations considered, we reiterated our recommendation in prior years
that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Inventory Committee to conduct the complete annual physical inventory of the
entire properties owned by the Municipality for all funds. As to PPEs under the
Special Education Fund, coordinate with the property custodian of different
schools who were recipients of local school board properties to verify and
account for their existence, completeness and condition;
b. Municipal Accountant and Municipal General Services Office to reconcile their
records and to verify the existence of unaccounted properties under the General
Fund. Adhere to the procedural guidelines prescribed in COA Circular No. 2020-
006; and
c. Municipal Accountant to provide depreciation to property, plant and equipment
totaling ₱65,731,323.42 to fairly present the municipality’s financial statements.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to observe the disclosure requirements in the preparation of the financial
statements and disclose the reconciliation of Actual Amounts on a Comparable Basis
and Actual Amounts in the Financial Statements in the Notes, in accordance with the
IPSASs and Section 3.4 of COA Circular No. 2016-004 to assists users in
understanding the financial statements and to provide them with information relevant
to decision-making.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. require both the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to investigate
the origin and nature of the noted discrepancies and make the necessary
adjustments to their respective records. Reconciliation of the cashbook and
subsidiary ledger/general ledger balances shall be conducted at least quarterly in
order to ensure completeness of the recorded financial transactions and accuracy
of the cash account balances as of a certain period;
b. direct the Municipal Accountant to consistently verify and review the balances
reflected in the SL/GL, which ultimately serves as the basis for the preparation of
the financial statements, for accuracy of records;
c. compel the Municipal Accountant to cause the preparation and submission of the
monthly Bank Reconciliation Statement for all depository accounts of the
Municipality upon receipt of the Bank Statements;
d. instruct the Municipal Treasurer to send written notice to the payees of the checks
which have become stale, notify them of the existence of checks and require
submission of the required documentation to facilitate issuance of new checks; and
e. direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to
cancel the payment recorded for the stale checks amounting to ₱483,994.64, and
restore the said amount to the cash and appropriate liability accounts. Remove
the stale checks from the list of outstanding checks in the bank reconciliation
statements and furnish the Municipal Treasurer with a copy of the JEV as her
basis for debiting the amount of stale checks in the Cashbook - Cash in Bank.

Foregoing considered, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Bids and Awards Committee to make sure that contract duration or time and all
other the relevant conditions are stipulated in all the contract agreements to
determine the extent of claims for liquidated damages in case of incurrence of
delay due to the fault of the contractors.
b. Municipal Accountant to observe the proper treatment of advance payment and
other pertinent accounting entries following the provisions of GAM for LGUs;
c. Municipal Engineer to cause submission of duly approved documents to support
the additional work rendered and the imposed suspension orders, including the
notices of resumption for the subject procurement projects. Submit copy of any
variation orders, be it change order or extra work order with reference to an
ongoing project implementation to the concerned COA auditor immediately upon
execution and approval thereof.
d. Municipal Accountant to ensure that disbursements are properly supported with
complete documentation. Discontinue the practice of processing the payment for
claims that are not sufficiently documented;
e. Municipal Engineer to submit suspension/extension and resumption orders for
delayed projects, if warranted. Otherwise, impose correct liquidated damages
against the contractor.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Bids and Awards Committee
(BAC), Municipal General Services Officer, and Municipal Accountant to submit the
lacking documentary requirements to the COA Office for evaluation of the Audit
Team.
Prospectively, the BAC, Municipal General Services Officer and Municipal Accountant
to ensure that all subsequent procurement projects are supported with properly
accomplished and signed documents, incorporating therein the complete information
and details in compliance with the existing laws, rules and regulations.

Based on the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to stop the practice of procuring goods from cash advances,
except for petty cash expenses in accordance with applicable laws, rules and
regulations;
b. Bids and Awards Committee to strictly adhere to the provisions of the 2016 RIRR
of RA No. 9184 on the use of the alternative mode of procurement, if competitive
bidding is not viable;
c. concerned Municipal personnel to cause submission of the lacking documents
supporting the liquidation reports for further evaluation of the Audit Team;
d. Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of documents supporting the
disbursement vouchers before making any payment; and
e. Municipal Accountant to record the cost of the freezer amounting to ₱18,594.00 as
equipment and henceforth, ensure that proper accounts are used in recording the
assets acquired and expenses incurred by the Municipality.

In view of the foregoing, the Audit Team recommended that the Municipal Mayor
instruct the Municipal Accountant to observe the proper journal entries for recording
of receipts and disbursements under the Trust Fund. Refrain from making shortcut or
omission of entries for proper monitoring of the balances of the accounts in the
financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
cause the immediate submission of accounts totaling ₱701,772.81. Ensure adherence
to the prescribed schedule on the submission of disbursement vouchers, including
all supporting documents as required by laws, rules and regulations and for timely
verification of the completed and/or paid transactions of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Social Welfare and
Development Officer to submit written justification on the prolonged delay in the
submission of the accounts, and require the immediate submission of the liquidation
reports, supporting documents and related accounting reports pertaining to the
₱41,840,500.00 funds of the Social Amelioration Program (SAP) received by the
Municipality from DSWD in CY 2020.

Foregoing considered, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Bids and Awards Committee to strictly abide to the 2016 RIRR of RA No. 9184 and
ensure that no award of contract shall be made until the applicable appropriations
ordinance has been approved or enacted;
b. Municipal Accountant to adhere to the accounting policies whereby creation of
special account shall be made through the maintenance of necessary subsidiary
ledger and preparation of appropriate reportorial requirements; and
c. Municipal Accountant to refrain from recording the procured inventory items as an
outright expense and direct deduction to a liability account. Instead, Inventory
accounts shall be first recognized upon delivery while issuances thereof shall be
recorded as they take place to be supported by Requisition and Issue Slip.
Accordingly, the corresponding liability account shall be reduced by the amount
expended upon liquidation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to:
a. issue demand letters to notify: (1) concerned officials and employees of their
responsibility to settle their unliquidated cash advances. If settlement was
not received, withhold from their salaries considering that they have been
notified and given more adequate time to liquidate; (2) employees who
already retired, resigned and transferred to immediately cause the liquidation
or settlement of the unliquidated balances;
b. request the write-off of the unliquidated cash advances balance of the
deceased employee;
c. refrain from granting additional cash advance to concerned officials and
employees unless the previous cash advance granted to them had been
liquidated; and
d. strictly monitor periodically the submission of liquidation reports to ensure
timely accounting thereof to avoid accumulation of outstanding cash
advances.

To ascertain the appropriate audit action that the Audit Team may take, we
recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned Municipal officials to
submit sufficient legal basis on the grant of the anniversary bonus to the Municipal
officials and employees. Otherwise, said disbursements totaling ₱333,000.00 may be
considered irregular expenditures being contrary to the provisions of LBC No. 65 and
COA Circular No. 2012-003.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. concerned officials to ensure that all forms of financial assistance provided by
the Municipality are approved by the Sangguniang Bayan and supported with
written guidelines for the grant of financial assistance which define, among
others, the qualifications of beneficiaries, purpose of the grant, amount to be
granted and the documentary requirements to serve as basis and control tool
in the implementation of various programs;
b. disbursing officer and Municipal Accountant to ensure that the payroll is
properly signed by each claimant or his/her duly authorized representative
with a special power of attorney (SPA) or other form of authority from the
claimant;
c. Municipal Accountant to ensure that liquidation reports are supported with
complete and appropriate supporting documents;
d. Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments to correct the
recording of the liquidation and restore the balance of ₱22,500.00 in the
Advances for Operating Expenses account; and
e. Municipal Treasurer to immediately settle the unliquidated balance of
₱507,000.00.

In view thereof, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. MENRO and Municipal Accountant to submit Accomplishment Reports/Service
Records and Trip Passes for the hauling activities for the year 2020, for further
evaluation of the Audit Team;
b. Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and other concerned officials to take the
necessary procedures to correct or remedy the defects noted in the contract.
Include in future contracts all the required information listed in COA Circular No.
2012-001 and the pertinent Procurement regulations to protect the interests of the
Municipal government;
c. MENRO to adopt controls to monitor the Contractor’s compliance with the terms
and conditions of the contract, such as, but not limited to, formally appointing or
assigning competent employees to oversee and document the garbage collection
and validate or certify as to the correctness of the Statement of Accounts
submitted by the Contractor and indicate the actual volume of garbage hauled in
the Inspection and Acceptance Reports; and
d. MENRO to require the assigned personnel to prepare a Record of
Attendance/Service of the Contractor indicating the date, driver’s full name, type
of vehicle, plate number, time in, time out, and signature of the driver and Certified
Correct by the MENRO to provide assurance of dates of disposal and hauling
services.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to ensure the timely,
effective and efficient implementation of the approved priority development
projects within target completion period;
b. Municipal Development Council to conduct in-depth planning for prioritization of
PPAs in the 20 percent Development Fund by the consensus of the constituents
and in coordination with the MPDC to avoid realignment and sudden change of
projects intended for current year’s implementation.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Designate One (1) dedicated personnel under the supervision of the General Services Officer- ICO to continue the count of all PPE items by account without interruption until the PPE accounts are completed; and
b. Require the GSO-ICO and Municipal Accountant to reconcile the results of inventory per inventory per account and make the necessary adjustments to their respective records, if any.
observations.managementAction: personnel under the supervision of the General Services Officer- the General Services Office ICO to continue the count of all PPE items by account until completed; were assigned to assist the GSO-ICO in the conduct of physical count.
observations.reason: The Municipal records of the GSO-ICO and Municipal Accountant to reconcile the results of inventory per inventory per reconciled as of December 31, 2020. However, Municipal records were still not reconciled as of December 31, 2020. Hence, reiterated in Observation No. 1, Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to observe the disclosure requirements in the preparation of the financial statements and disclose the reconciliation of Actual Amounts on a Comparable Basis and Actual Amounts in the Financial Statements in the Notes, in accordance with the IPSASs and Section 3.4 of COA Circular No. 2016-004 to assists users in understanding the financial statements and to provide them with information relevant to decision-making.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was requested to observe the disclosure requirements in the preparation of the CY 2021 financial statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a. Municipal Treasurer to send written notice to the payees of the checks which have become stale; and, b. Municipal Accountant to prepare the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) for adjustments to the cash in bank and payable accounts for any unreleased check at year-end.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer sent written notice to payees of the checks which have become stale. The Municipal Accountant prepared the JEVs for adjustments to the cash in bank and payable accounts at the close of CY 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Assessor to submit Monthly Report of Assessment of Real Properties and Individual Tax Payers to the Municipal Treasurer for reconciliation of their records; and b. Municipal Accountant to maintain the subsidiary ledgers/schedule for the RPT and SET Receivables to facilitate the reconciliation with the records of the Municipal Treasurer at the end of each year.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer conducted reconciliation with the Assessor and adjusted the records accordingly. The Assessor and Treasurer coordinated to reconcile their records. The Municipal Treasurer prepared the list of delinquent taxpayers as of December 31, 2019 and submitted upon request of the Audit Team on June 21, 2021.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant was still in the process of reconciling the records; some records were only prepared upon request of the Audit Team and submitted on June 21, 2021, months after the books for CY 2020 were already closed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to: a. issue demand letters to notify concerned officials and employees to settle their unliquidated cash advances and withhold from salaries if not settled; b. request the write-off of unliquidated cash advances balance of deceased employee; c. refrain from granting additional cash advance unless previous cash advances are liquidated; and d. strictly monitor periodic submission of liquidation reports to avoid accumulation of outstanding cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were issued to officials and employees connected with the recommendation. The Municipal Accountant made adjustments to correct the recording of the liquidation to restore the balance of ₱6,000.00 in the Advances for Operating Expenses account. Cash advances were granted only to permanent employees of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Demand letters were issued to some officials and employees but not to all (those who already retired, resigned or transferred). No formal request for write-off was forwarded to the Audit Team for deceased employees. Some long-outstanding advances remained and liquidation for some accounts was still pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. require the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to ensure optimal utilization of funds for priority projects under AM and to implement the projects; b. present programmed projects in itemized form in the Annual Budget; and c. Municipal Engineer to submit necessary evaluation reports and certifications for time extensions.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) implemented some projects and ensured some projects were completed in CY 2020. Some documents and certifications were being collated by the Municipal Engineer for submission to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Some projects were implemented while others were delayed due to incomplete documentation and inability to obtain required certifications; difficulty in obtaining source documents for older projects; and limited time and resources.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the head of the Business Processing and Licensing Office and the IT Programmer to coordinate with DICT to check the program and ensure it properly reflected the rates and computations mandated by the revenue code of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor and IT Programmer reviewed the revenue code and the program to ensure that the system generated proper computation of business taxes. Adjustments were validated and corrected in the system.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to set-up and maintain Special Accounts in the General Fund for the monitoring of operating performance and viability of the Municipality’s economic enterprises and 20% Development Fund, and to record all transactions pertaining to these enterprises in the corresponding subsidiary ledgers.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant set up Special Accounts in the General Fund for the 20% Development Fund and maintained subsidiary ledgers for the monitoring of the fund.
observations.reason: Special Accounts in the General Fund for the 20% Development Fund were set-up but still required further detailing and consistent maintenance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC with assistance of the BAC Secretariat to submit the approved Procurement Monitoring Report to the GPPB in printed and electronic format within the prescribed period in accordance with the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The BAC prepared the Procurement Monitoring Report and submitted it to the GPPB.
observations.reason: The Procurement Monitoring Report was submitted to the GPPB; however, some procedural aspects required further refinement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct: a. MPDC to ensure timely, effective and efficient implementation of the approved priority development projects within target completion period; b. MPDC to refrain from presenting projects in lump sum and instead itemize; c. Municipal Engineer to submit necessary evaluation reports and certifications for time extensions.
observations.managementAction: The MPDC implemented 79.43% of the PPAs funded by the 20% DF. Some projects were implemented while others remained ongoing as of year-end. The Municipal Engineer provided some evaluation reports and certifications.
observations.reason: Some projects were implemented while others were delayed due to late award of contracts (December), inadequate planning, and the need to collate additional documentation for projects. Time extensions required supporting evaluations which were not always available.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. MDRRM Officer to ensure that future programs and activities charged to the 5% LDRRMF are aligned with priority projects specified in Section 5 of the JMC; b. Municipal Accountant to refund unexpended balances of completed projects to source agencies; c. Municipal Accountant to record purchased PPE in the General Fund books and refrain from shortcut entries.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRM Officer provided financial assistance and details for the PPAs charged to the LDRRMF and coordinated with the Municipal Accountant on recording of PPE purchased. Some items of PPE were taken up in the records and adjustments were made.
observations.reason: Some PPAs were realigned and funded under LDRRMF; however, documentation for claims was incomplete and some PPE recognition still required adjustments. There were difficulties obtaining complete documentation for older items.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to refund the amount of ₱14,863.65 incurred for unnecessary expenses.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer refunded the amount of unnecessary expenses where applicable and followed up for other refunds.
observations.reason: Some amounts were refunded to the Treasurer’s Office; however, full compliance and reconciliation for all items required additional verification and adjustments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to include among her priorities the construction of storeroom that can accommodate all the documents generated by the municipality and its 42 barangays to be preserved for a period of ten (10) years as mandated by existing government auditing laws; and that the Municipal Budget Officer appropriate funds for the construction of storeroom.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Planning and Development Officer included the storeroom project among priorities and planned for inclusion in future budgets. No construction was undertaken as of the report.
observations.reason: The matter was not prioritized due to competing demands on space and funding; the concerned units had not yet procured or constructed the storeroom and funding was not yet appropriated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to require the elected officials to submit the prescribed official receipt of the Republic of the Philippines in relation to the payment of their annual dues and other fees paid to their respective leagues.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant requested elected officials to submit official receipts for payments of annual dues and membership fees. The Municipality continued to use customized official receipts of leagues as attachments to disbursement vouchers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant adhere strictly to the aforesaid PPSAS and properly identify/classify the current and non-current portion of the long-term loans payable in the financial statements and draw the necessary journal entries to reflect proper classification.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant properly classified the current and non-current portions of loans according to PPSAS and made the necessary adjustments in the accounting records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MENRO to implement strictly the provisions of RA 9003 and to intensify the 'No Segregation, No Collection' policy at barangay level, and the Solid Waste Management Board to monitor and encourage compliance by barangays and provide financial assistance to barangays desiring to have their own MRF.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality exerted efforts to comply with RA 9003 and provided some financial assistance to barangays requesting to establish their own MRFs. Some barangays submitted resolutions requesting assistance; initial financial assistance was provided to a few barangays.
observations.reason: There were barangays with inactive MRFs and ongoing efforts to implement segregation and to provide financial assistance; full compliance yet to be achieved.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer to demand the contractor to immediately complete the project, impose sanctions and penalties, compute the amount of liquidated damages and deduct the same from payments due to the contractor.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer demanded the contractor complete the project and imposed certain compliance requests. However, the project was used as an evacuation center during the Taal Volcano eruption and later served as an isolation facility for COVID-19, which affected resumption of works.
observations.reason: The project remained suspended as the school facility was repurposed as evacuation and isolation center; hence, completion was delayed and liquidated damages were not fully imposed as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to submit monthly report of assessment and to reconcile with the Municipal Treasurer; and the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers and reconcile balances.
observations.managementAction: The Assessor and Municipal Treasurer coordinated and conducted reconciliations for some periods; door-to-door verifications and serving of statements of accounts for taxpayers were undertaken for some taxpayers.
observations.reason: Not all statement of accounts were served and some taxpayers could not be located; reconciliation was ongoing and was constrained by manpower and time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Provincial Accountant to reconcile the receivables/payables and to obtain information for long outstanding accounts. If found to have no valid claimant revert to surplus for closing.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant coordinated with the Provincial Accountant; some reconciliations were undertaken and certain balances were validated. The Municipal Accountant also coordinated with concerned officials to obtain information on outstanding accounts.
observations.reason: There were still unreconciled balances and some accounts outstanding for many years; further coordination with the Province and additional documentation were needed to finalize adjustments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to review the accounts payable, identify invalid obligations, revert the unclaimed/unrequired balances to unappropriated surplus, and ensure proper documentation is submitted for audit.
observations.managementAction: Some of the accounts payable were reviewed and SB Resolution was enacted to revert certain amounts to surplus. The Municipal Accountant initiated review of long outstanding accounts.
observations.reason: Not all obligations could be validated; older records were incomplete and required further verification. Some reversions were enacted but full completion awaited additional documentation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended refunding the amount of ₱14,863.65 which was used for unnecessary expenditures.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer refunded amounts where applicable and adjustments were being coordinated.
observations.reason: Some refunds were made to the Treasurer’s Office but full reconciliation pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MPDC to include construction of a storeroom among priorities and the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate funds for the construction.
observations.managementAction: MPDC included the storeroom project as priority for future budgeting. No construction was undertaken as of the report.
observations.reason: No funds were yet appropriated and the project was not prioritized due to other demands; concerned units had not yet constructed the storeroom.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Accountant require elected officials to submit official receipts of the Republic of the Philippines for the payment of annual dues and other fees paid to their respective leagues.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant requested and obtained required documents where possible and required officials to submit official receipts for their payments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: • The Commission on Audit Technical Audit Specialists (TAS) to closely monitor and institute corrective measures on the findings of COA-TAS and immediately inform the audit team of the courses of actions taken by the contractor. Otherwise, demand the payment from the contractor the amount of ₱491,563.93.
observations.managementAction: No action taken yet on the findings to demand the payment from the contractor.
observations.reason: The concerned officials did not implement the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to disclose the reconciliation of Actual Amounts on a Comparable Basis and Actual Amounts in the Financial Statements in the Notes, in accordance with the PPSASs and Section 3.4 of COA Circular No. 2016-004 to assist users in understanding the financial statements and to provide them with information relevant to decision-making.
observations.managementAction: No action taken yet. Reiterated in Observation No. 2, Page No. 36.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: • Monitor implementation to ensure immediate compliance; • Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate funds for expenses to be incurred relative to the construction of storeroom and ensure the project is included in Annual Budget; • Municipal Engineer to supervise the implementation; and • Municipal Accountant to ensure proper recording of project disbursements.
observations.managementAction: No action taken yet to provide adequate storeroom space. The matter was not prioritized by concerned units.
observations.reason: The matter was not prioritized by the concerned units; no funds appropriated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to attach official receipts of the Republic of the Philippines in relation to the payment of their annual dues, membership fees and other fees paid to government organizations.
observations.managementAction: No action taken. The municipality still used customized or other receipts for the leagues as supporting documents.
observations.reason: The municipality still used customized or other receipts; matters pending compliance.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the material discrepancy of the PPE accounts and continuous failure of the
Municipality to correct such deficiencies, we reiterated our recommendations that the
Municipal Mayor:
a. Designate dedicated personnel under the supervision of the General Services
Officer-ICO to continue without interruption the count of all PPE items by account
until completed; and,
b. Require the GSO-ICO and Municipal Accountant to immediately reconcile the
results of inventory per PPE account and make the necessary adjustments to their
respective records, if any.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
observe the disclosure requirements in the preparation of the financial statements and
disclose the reconciliation of Actual Amounts on a Comparable Basis and Actual
Amounts in the Financial Statements in the Notes, in accordance with the PPSASs and
Section 3.4 of COA Circular No. 2016-004 to assists users in understanding the
financial statements and to provide them with information relevant to decision-making.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to send written notice to the payees of the checks which have
become stale, notify them of the existence of checks and require submission of the
required documentation to facilitate issuance of new checks; and,
b. Municipal Accountant to prepare the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) for adjustments
to the cash in bank and payable accounts for any unreleased check at year-end. In
the ensuing year, prepare a JEV to reverse the entries made by debiting the payable
accounts and crediting Cash in Bank-Local Currency Current Account.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to provide
the Municipal Accountant with a complete list of delinquent taxpayers with their
corresponding amount payable excluding penalty as of December 31, 2019. Thereafter,
the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjusting entries to recognize the
correct Real Property Tax Receivables and Special Education Tax Receivables in the
books of accounts and utilize the said list in maintaining subsidiary ledgers/schedule
to facilitate reconciliation with the records of the Municipal Treasurer at the end of each
year.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

A. Require the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Issue demand letters to notify: (1) concerned officials and employees of their
responsibility to settle their unliquidated cash advances. If settlement was not
received, withhold from their salaries considering that they have been notified and
given more adequate time to liquidate; (2) employees who already retired,
resigned and transferred to immediately cause the liquidation or settlement of the
unliquidated balances;
b. Request the write-off of the unliquidated cash advances balance of the deceased
employee;
c. Refrain from granting additional cash advance to concerned officials and
employees unless the previous cash advance granted to them had been
liquidated;
d. Strictly monitor periodically the submission of liquidation reports to ensure
timely accounting thereof to avoid accumulation of outstanding cash advances;
e. Make the necessary adjustments to correct the double recording of the
liquidation to restore the balance of ₱6,000.00 in the Advances for Operating
Expenses account.

B. Discontinue the practice of granting cash advances to elected officials for purposes
other than official traveling expenses. Instead, these cash advances should be
undertaken by accountable officers duly designated for the purpose and sufficiently
bonded to cover such accountability.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:

A. Municipal GSO to:
a. Number each type of supplies and maintain Stock Cards per stock number;
b. Forward copy of the Acceptance and Inspection Report (AIR) to the Municipal
Accountant for the preparation of the DV and recording of deliveries in the
appropriate ledger cards;
c. Utilize and consolidate weekly the Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) for which
supplies and materials were issued using the Summary of Supplies and Materials
Issued (SSMI) and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant together with
the original copies of RIS; and
d. Use the prescribed format in reporting the physical count of inventories and
submit the same to the Office of the Auditor not later than July 31 and January 31
of each year in coordination with the Inventory Committee.

B. Municipal Accountant to:
a. Maintain Supplies Ledger Cards per stock number and record the purchases in
the appropriate ledger cards upon receipt of the AIR and other supporting
documents;
b. Use the appropriate inventory account in recording the purchases and
corresponding expense account upon receipt of SSMI submitted by the GSO; and,
c. Use the moving average method in costing the inventories.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Require the Municipal Planning and Development Officer in coordination with other
municipal officials concerned to faithfully monitor the receipt of funds and evaluate
the implementation of the projects to avoid unnecessary delays;
b. Cause the immediate implementation of the programmed infrastructure projects to
allow the early enjoyment of benefits by the general public; and,
c. Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to immediately
refund/return the unexpended balances of the completed projects to source
agencies.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the head of the Business
Processing and Licensing Office and the IT Programmer to coordinate immediately
with the DICT to check the possible cause of the discrepancies noted in the
computations generated by the system. Ensure that the basis of computation of
business taxes is aligned with the latest Revenue Code of the Municipality such that
the system will generate a reliable and accurate computation of business taxes.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to set-up
and maintain Special Accounts in the General Fund for the monitoring of operating
performance and viability of the Municipality’s economic enterprises and 20%
Development Fund, and to serve as a decision-making tool of the agency. Also, record
all transactions pertaining to these economic enterprises to the corresponding
subsidiary ledgers.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Bids and Awards Committee
(BAC) with the assistance of the BAC Secretariat to submit the approved Procurement
Monitoring Report to the GPPB in printed and electronic format within the prescribed
period in accordance with the provision of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. MPDC Officer to ensure optimal utilization of funds for priority projects under the
20 percent DF to enable the Municipality and its constituents to reap the socio-
economic benefits at the earliest possible time;
b. MPDC Officer to refrain from presenting projects in lump sum, rather, present the
PPAs to be funded by the 20 percent DF on a per project and per Barangay basis
together with its corresponding estimated cost for itemized appropriation in the
Annual and Supplemental Budget; and
c. MEO to submit the necessary documents, evaluation report and certifications for
the granted time extensions.

We recommended that the Mayor direct the:
a. MDRRM Officer to ensure that future programs and activities charged to the 5%
LDRRMF are aligned with the priority projects and activities specified in Section 5
of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1;
b. Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documents/certifications noted in
Annex U and ensure that claims are supported with complete documentation before
approving payment;
c. Municipal Accountant to record the purchased PPE in the General Fund and refrain
from making shortcut entry for proper monitoring of accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Local School Board to evaluate
carefully the programs and projects identified during the year in accordance with the
provisions stated in Section 272 of the RA 7160 and DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint
Circular No. 01 s. 2017 dated January 19, 2017 and cause its efficient and effective
implementation of all projects and projects in order to achieve the optimal provision of
quality education in the public schools.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person and TWG
Chairperson to the comply with the schedule to be observed in GAD Planning and
Budgeting as provided by PCW-DBM-DILG-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as amended by
PCW-DBM-DILG-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01 to facilitate timely submission to the PPDO
and subsequent review and endorsement by the DILG Provincial Office.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Remit in full the taxes withheld from payments within 10 days following the month in
which such withholding was made, in accordance with the deadline prescribed by the
BIR for filing and remittance of the said taxes; and,
b. Identify the cause/s of under and over remittance for some months, and reconcile the
amount of taxes actually withheld and remitted with the year-end balance of Due to
BIR reported in the financial statements, and adjust the records accordingly.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: • the GSO/Inventory Committee, after physical count of properties shall prepare the Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) thereon with complete information and submit the same to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of each year; • the GSO/Inventory Committee to prepare the inventory list by category and complete a physical count and perform the actual and reconcile the results of the count with the accounting records and establish the correct balance in the Financial Statements; • the General Services Officer to update the Property Cards in order to provide the balances per card in the RPCPPE and immediately identified the missing properties or discrepancies, if any, of the PPEs on record against the actual count. • Likewise, Acknowledgement Receipt of Equipment (ARE) for PPE’s acquired on prior years should be updated. Ensure that the Acknowledgement Receipt of Equipment was renewed at least every three years or whenever there is a change in custodianship to establish proper accountability; • the Municipal Accountant to prepare the lapsing schedule with complete information consistent with the balances presented in the financial statements for CY 2019; • the Municipal Accountant to trace properties without supporting documents and provide the corresponding depreciation expenses; and • the Municipal Accountant to update the required Property Plant and Equipment Ledger Card to facilitate the reconciliation of RPCPPE with the accounting records.
observations.managementAction: The GSO has conducted partial physical count and placing stickers on the PPE items. The GSO is still updating the property cards. The Municipal Accountant submitted an updated lapsing schedule consistent with the balances presented in the financial statements for CY 2019. The Municipal Accountant acknowledged the liquidation documents received from various employees by signing the Liquidation Reports and when such reports was received for proper monitoring.
observations.reason: Lack of dedicated staff to complete the conduct of physical count. Lack of manpower to perform the required tasks.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Cash Advances totaling ₱2,613,038.63 not obligated as evidenced by attachment of Obligation Request for all disbursements including cash advances that were granted by the Municipality except disbursements in the Trust Fund. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant check the attached supporting documents before signing the disbursement voucher as to its obligated allotment and completeness of the supporting documents. b. Cash advances totaling ₱1,225,195.40 were made for the payment of various transactions, thus evaded the required procurement and process. Likewise, such mode of payment resulted to non-withholding of taxes contrary to Revenue Regulation No. 2-98 dated May 17, 1998 thus, depriving the government of additional income. We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer refrain from granting cash advances to accountable officers which payments can be made directly to the supplier/contractor in order for the Municipality to deduct and withhold applicable taxes and remit the same in accordance with the BIR Rules and Regulations. c. Existence of Unliquidated Cash Advance totaling ₱700,009.61 as of year-end and Delays in Liquidation for Cash Advances totaling ₱8,973,144.26 and Liquidation Reports for Cash Advances totaling ₱1,769,580.00 not signed by the Municipal Accountant.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer certifies the existence of available appropriation as evidenced by preparation of Obligation Request for all disbursements including cash advances that were granted by the Municipality. The Municipal Accountant checked the supporting documents before signing the disbursement voucher as to its obligated allotment and completeness of the supporting documents. In compliance with the issued Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2020-06, the Municipal Accountant served demand letters to the concerned accountable officers for the immediate settlement of their outstanding cash advances, and he informed the Audit Team that elected officials would no longer be granted cash advances for purposes other than official traveling expenses. Further, the Municipal Accountant added that Journal Entry No. 100-2020-01-14436 was drawn to correct the double recording of the liquidation in the amount of ₱6,000.00.
observations.reason: Lack of dedicated personnel to perform the required tasks; granting of cash advances to accountable officers for purchases was minimized; some procurements remain to be fully regularized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: • the Municipal Accountant prepare the journal entry for the transfer of the unexpended LDRRMF balance of the 70% Mitigation Fund- MOOE and the 30% QRF as of year-end to Trust Liability-DRRMF; • the Municipal Budget Officer verify the existence of appropriations for a certain project before issuing/signing the Obligation Request; • the Municipal Treasurer in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer effect the transfer of funds amounting to ₱1,045,000.00 to the General Fund; and • the MDRRMO ensure that the Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF is compliant with COA Circular 2012-002 and that the amounts reflected in the report is fair so as not to mislead the users of the report.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared the JEV no. 300-2019-03-008 dated March 11, 2019. The Municipal Budget Officer verifies the existence of appropriations for a certain project before issuing/signing the Obligation Request. The Municipal Officials effected the transfer of funds amounting to ₱1,045,000.00 to the General Fund on June 24, 2019. The Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF was submitted in accordance with the prescribed format.

observations.recommendation: Based on the foregoing observations, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MENRO to strictly implement the provisions of RA 9003 and intensify the “No Segregation, No Collection Policy” among every barangay; the Solid Waste Management Board to monitor and encourage financial assistance to barangays desiring to have their own MRF; and the Municipality to provide financial assistance to barangays and discontinue the practice of allowing monthly deductions of observed the GAA provision that monthly take home pay of employee shall not be lower than Five Thousand Pesos (₱5,000.00).
observations.managementAction: The Municipality is implementing efforts to comply with the mandates of RA 9003. The Municipality is providing financial assistance to barangays desiring MRF. Information dissemination and barangay activities were conducted and the No Segregation, No Collection Policy was implemented. The Solid Waste Management Board is monitoring compliance. The Municipality conducted environmental campaigns and other interventions.
observations.reason: The municipality is exerting efforts but there is need for more manpower and continued enforcement and funding support; some barangays remain inactive and need further support.

observations.recommendation: In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: • Bids and Awards Committee to observe the provisions of the IRR of RA 9184 in the posting of Notice of Award and Notice to Proceed together with the approved contract in the agency website and PhilGEPS. • Municipal Engineer to put on record the date when the projects completed are accepted by the Municipality. Likewise, to carefully plan the contract duration of infrastructure works considering the preparation of programs of works for a just and fair labor cost. Also, compute the amount of Liquidated damages to be deducted on the payments to be made to the contractor.
observations.managementAction: Based on interview, the BAC were observing the provisions of the IRR of R.A. 9184 in the posting of Notice of Award and Notice to Proceed together with the approved contract in the agency website and PhilGEPS. The Municipal Engineer demanded the contractor the immediate completion of the project through a letter dated August 5, 2019 and received by the Contractor on August 6, 2019. However, the project was suspended as of December 31, 2019. The Municipal Engineer is now monitoring the date when the projects are completed and accepted by the Municipality.
observations.reason: The project was not yet completed as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: • Bids and Awards Committee to observe the provisions of the IRR of RA 9184 in the posting of Notice of Award and Notice to Proceed together with the approved contract in the agency website and PhilGEPS and require the BAC Secretariat to issue certification on the posting of the aforementioned documents in any conspicuous places in the premises of the procuring entity; and • Municipal Engineer to put on record the date when projects completed are accepted by the Municipality. Likewise, to carefully plan the contract duration of infrastructure works and prepare the program of works for a just and fair labor cost.
observations.managementAction: Based on interview, the BAC were observing the provisions of the IRR of RA 9184 in the posting of Notice of Award and Notice to Proceed together with the approved contract in the agency website and PhilGEPS. The BAC Secretariat is issuing certification on the posting of the aforementione d documents in any conspicuous places in the premises of the Municipality. The Municipal Engineer is now monitoring the date when the projects are accepted by the Municipality.
observations.reason: The municipality is observing procurement posting requirements and documents; the Municipal Engineer is monitoring implementation but full documentation and posting certifications were being completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC consolidate the purchase requests received from various offices and to procure the same in accordance with the requirements of RA 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality consolidates purchase requests received from various offices/departments and procure the same in accordance with the intent and requirements of RA 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Office of the Human Resource Management and Development and the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to discontinue the practice that allows the monthly loan deductions observed and strictly comply with the GAA provision that monthly take home pay of employee shall not be lower than Five Thousand Pesos (₱5,000.00).
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Management complied. The municipality is now enforcing GAA provision and stopped unauthorized monthly deductions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Local Finance Committee, the Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to regularly monitor the cash availability of the Municipality so as to avoid the incurrence of continuous cash overdraft.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Finance Committee, Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer were monitoring the cash availability of the Municipality to avoid the incurrence of cash overdraft.
observations.reason: Municipality still incurred a cash insufficiency in the Trust Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the following: • Municipal Accountant and Assessor to reconcile their records to Assessor’s Office by comparing individual tax declaration and tax assessment records; • Municipal Assessor to submit Monthly Report of Tax Assessment and Individual Tax Payers Assessment to the Municipal Treasurer for reconciliation; • Municipal Treasurer to exhaust all efforts and devise strategies to collect all the real delinquent property taxes and encourage the taxpayer to pay their dues on time. Conduct verification on those outstanding balances to determine the accuracy of the receivables and its collectability.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Municipal Assessor conducted reconciliation to the Assessor’s Office by comparing the records. The Municipal Treasurer conducted door to door visits and served Statement of Accounts to taxpayers and coordinated with the Assessor’s Office for reconciliation. Municipal Treasurer planned tax information campaign and coordination with Barangays.
observations.reason: Limited manpower; reconciliation not yet 100% complete; some taxpayers could not be located; serving of statements and full reconciliation continued in 2018/2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Coordinate again with the Province and the concerned officials and departments to obtain information on other long outstanding accounts. b. Coordinate with the concerned officials and departments to obtain information on the validity of the outstanding accounts. If found to have no valid claims, revert to surplus the accounts and consider as invalid/undocumented obligation as required under Section 98 of P.D. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant coordinated again with the Province to reconcile receivables/payables however there were still unreconciled balances. The Municipal Accountant validated existence of receivables/payables and pursued reconciliation. The Municipal Accountant prepared request letters and coordination with concerned local officials and departments.
observations.reason: Limited time and other priority tasks resulted to incomplete reconciliation; some balances remain unreconciled because of lack of supporting documents and long outstanding nature.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the General Services Office to conduct the physical count of inventories on a semestral basis and prepare the RPCI and submit copies to the Municipal Accountant and to the Audit Team within the prescribed period. Reconcile the results of the count with the accounting records and establish the causes of discrepancies, whether items had already been missing, donated, sold or consumed to arrive at the correct balance in the Financial Statements. Also to maintain stock cards; and that the Accounting Department reconcile with the submitted RPCI of the GSO.
observations.managementAction: The GSO prepared inventory listing of all supplies with quantity as of December 31, 2019. The Municipal Accountant reconciled partially the RPCI submitted by the GSO. Some supplies ledger cards were prepared by the Accounting Office though incomplete. The GSO conducted partial inventory and prepared some listings.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower to perform required tasks; no complete RPCI for prior years; supplies ledger cards not fully maintained.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the GSO Inventory Committee to prepare inventory list by category and complete a physical count of PPE and perform the actual count and reconcile the results with the accounting records and establish the correct balance in the Financial Statements; prepare and maintain the property cards and update ARE; the Accounting Department to identify the PPEs included in the beginning/unidentified balances and to secure documents to support the PPEs; the Chairman of the Committee on Disposal to initiate/facilitate the disposal of unserviceable properties; and the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries in the books of accounts upon disposal of the said property.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee headed by the Municipal GSO prepared inventory listing of all PPE and performed the physical count for PPE purchased during CY 2018-2019. ARE’s for CY 2018-2019 acquisitions required were updated for PPE acquisitions during CY 2018-2019. Disposal requests were submitted and some disposals were already made and submitted. The Municipal Accountant prepared some required adjusting entries.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower to identify the PPEs included in the beginning/ unidentified balances and to secure supporting documents; only AREs for CY 2018-2019 were updated; many prior year PPE ledger cards still to be updated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the documents pertaining to the unpaid obligations to COA for verification and review. If found to have no valid claims or obligations, cause the reversion of the same to the unappropriated surplus.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted some documents to COA and validated the claims. Some supporting documents were no longer available for prior years’ payables and those amounts could not be validated. The Municipal Accountant prepared the necessary documents for reversion where applicable.
observations.reason: Some supporting documents were no longer available; some unpaid claims were old and could not be validated and thus remain outstanding; reversion requests being prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to refrain from granting cash advances to accountable officers which payments can be made directly to the supplier/contractor and request refund of the amount of ₱14,863.65 incurred for unnecessary expenses and to ensure that future SEF expenditures are in line with the DepEd/DBM/DILG Joint Circular.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer refunded part of the unsubstantiated amount. The Municipal Accountant submitted liquidation and supporting documents for several SEF disbursements and refunded unnecessary expenses where applicable. Municipality discontinued practice of granting cash advances for purchases that can be made directly to suppliers.
observations.reason: Some disbursements were already made and not all funds refunded; some documents were submitted late; further monitoring required.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (MDRRM) to enforce implementation of the programs and projects under the 70% Mitigation Fund and have those programs and projects completed within the implementation time to avoid delay in the delivery of services and benefits to constituents.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality utilized funds for training, procurement of equipment and other mitigation activities. MDRRMC conducted trainings and implemented several mitigation activities and purchased equipment. The municipality utilized 97% of the 70% Mitigation Fund for CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the GAD Focal Person to: • Properly monitor and report regularly the status of implementation of the Gender and Development activities and have an updated GAD Database; • Update the GAD Database to have an accurate basis and strengthen GAD planning and budgeting; and • ensure that planned PPAs address identified gender issues.
observations.managementAction: The municipality implemented 70% or ₱8,175,822.52 out of ₱11,613,624.00 annual GAD Budget. Some GAD activities were implemented; information dissemination and activities were conducted. The Municipal Government included various offices and organizations to actively participate in planning and implementation of GAD activities. The GAD Database was being updated using CBMS data and PSA sources.
observations.reason: Some activities were not implemented due to lack of funds, non-availability of DILG for CBMS updating, and delayed approval of some programs; continued coordination and funding needed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure the optimal utilization of LCPC funds and instruct the Office of the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office to optimally utilize the allotted budget and implement more activities as enumerated in the DILG Memorandum No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Planning and Development Office and MSWDO implemented various activities and utilized LCPC funds. Various activities were charged and implemented in CY 2018 and some expenditures were charged to the Office of the Mayor. The municipality implemented some LCPC activities benefiting children.
observations.reason: Limited programs implemented; some funds used for other GAD/child-related activities; further optimizaton of LCPC activities required.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning and Development Office to coordinate with concerned department heads to enforce the utilization of the unexpended balance of ₱1,629,176.64 and ₱1,000,000.00. Also, require the Municipal Accountant to submit liquidation and documents and if unexpended funds are identified, return to source agencies.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Planning and Development Office coordinated with concerned offices and proposed projects for use of unexpended balances. The Municipal Accountant submitted liquidation and supporting documents for some items and processed return of unexpended balances where applicable. Some checks were processed and returned. Future funds to be fully implemented with COA audit team furnished copies of approved MOA.
observations.reason: Some balances were not yet refunded as documents were being gathered; some projects implemented in 2018; recovery/return of funds still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to set-up and maintain Special Accounts in the General Fund for the monitoring of operating performance and viability of the Municipality’s economic enterprises and 20% Development Fund, and to serve as a decision-making tool of the agency. Also, record all transactions pertaining to these economic enterprises to the corresponding subsidiary ledgers.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant commented that the market operated by the Municipality is not self-sustaining and is subsidized by Municipal funds. The Municipal Accountant promised to coordinate with the programmer of their accounting system to set-up special accounts for the economic enterprise and the 20% Development Fund.
observations.reason: Market subsidized by municipal funds; setup of special accounts pending system configuration and coordination with IT/programmer.

observations.recommendation: • The Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant set-up Special Accounts in the General Fund for the monitoring of the performance and viability of the Municipality’s economic enterprises and 20% Development Fund, and to serve as a decision making tool of the agency. Also, record all transactions pertaining to these economic enterprises to the corresponding subsidiary ledgers.
observations.managementAction: Implemented: The staff set up appropriate records and started monitoring the economic enterprises and 20% Development Fund as required.

observations.recommendation: • Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant require elected officials to submit the prescribed official receipt of the Republic of the Philippines in relation to the payment of their annual dues, membership fees and other fees paid to their respective leagues as attachment in the disbursement voucher.
observations.managementAction: Implemented: The Municipality ensured that official receipts and documentation are required for such payments and improved procedures for documentation of payments to leagues.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:
• the GSO/Inventory Committee, after physical count of properties shall prepare the
report (RPCPPE) thereon with complete information and submit the same to the
Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of each year;
• the GSO/Inventory Committee to compare the inventory listing with the property
