We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of all Property, Plant and Equipment at least once a year to ensure its existence, prepare the report (RPCPPE) thereon with complete information and submit to the Accounting Office for reconciliation. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the newly appointed General Services Officer to prepare and maintain Property Cards in order to provide the balances per card in the RPCPPE and immediately identified the missing properties or discrepancies, if any, of the PPEs on record against the actual count. Likewise, ensure that the Acknowledgement Receipt of Equipment was renewed at least every three years or whenever there is a change in custodianship to established proper accountability.

To ensure the accuracy, reliability and fair presentation of financial statements, we further recommended that the Municipal Mayor oblige the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Prepare the lapsing schedule with complete information consistent with the balances presented in the financial statements;
b. Trace the records of all PPEs and provide the corresponding account and depreciation expenses; and
c. Prepare and maintain the required Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card to facilitate the reconciliation of RPCPPE with the accounting records.
observations.managementAction:
Partially Implemented Committee conducted physical count of PPE. However, not all properties were subjected to physical count nor supported with RPCPPE.

Not Implemented The General Services Officer still failed to prepare and maintain Property Cards. Likewise, the Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment were not updated and renewed for at least three years as prescribed.

Partially Implemented The Municipal Accountant prepared lapsing schedule but not all PPE accounts have complete listing of PPE.

Not Implemented PPEs under Land Improvements and other beginning/unidentified balances remain not fully traced with appropriate depreciation and identification.
observations.reason: The General Services Officer just assumed office on May 2017 and is still in the process of adapting and learning procedures on property management; some beginning balances date back many years and lack supporting documents; insufficient manpower and turnover affected completeness of records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their respective reports so that their respective reports were properly guided in their decision making and avoid confusions to the users of their report. Meanwhile, the Cash in Treasury had a difference of ₱50.00 due to over but was immediately adjusted in 2018. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant stop the practice of recording reverting the unreleased checks to Cash and Accounts Payable for fair presentation of the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had conducted monthly reconciliation of their reported cash balances. This was evidenced by reconciled balances in Cash in Bank – LCCA, CA, SA and Time Deposit. Cash in Treasury had a difference of ₱50.00 due to over but was immediately adjusted in 2018. They also follow the audit recommendation regarding the reversion of unreleased checks to Cash and Accounts Payable.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

a. Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of all supplies semestrally and prepare the corresponding Report of Physical Count of Inventories and submit to the Accounting Office and Auditor concerned within the prescribed period;

b. Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer to prepare and maintain the Supplies Ledger Cards and Stock Cards, respectively; and

c. Municipal Officials concerned implement required requisition and issuance reporting and ensure purchases of supplies and materials are recorded to inventory accounts and not charged immediately to expense unless procured through petty cash for immediate use.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal GSO Officer and the Municipal Accountant had commented that they had scheduled physical count of inventories on June 30, 2018 and December 31, 2018. Likewise, the corresponding RPCI will be reconciled with the accounting records and submit to the audit team. They also assure that the GSO Officer will prepare and maintain the necessary stock cards while the accounting will provide the supplies ledger cards. As to the recording of inventories procured, they agreed on the recommendation to charge them on their proper inventory accounts and record expenses upon their issuance.
observations.reason: Inventory physical counts scheduled but not yet conducted at time of report; stock cards and supplies ledger cards not yet prepared; some purchases still recorded as expenses.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Local School Board, Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the laws, rules and regulations and ensure that SEF be utilized exclusively for the purpose for which it was created, and avoid charging SEF for expenditure items that are provided in DepEd budget (MOOE). We also recommended stopping the practice of allocating the five percent of SEF budget for LDRRMF which is not in accordance with Sections 100(c) and 272 of RA 7160, and require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to transfer corresponding cash balances of unexpended LDRRMF of SEF to Trust Fund books and prepare necessary adjusting entries.
observations.managementAction: Municipality agreed and transferred amounts (₱307,850.00 and ₱25,000.00) and adjusted to comply with prior year’s recommendation. They will ensure future budgeting adheres to JC No. 1 s. 2017 and DepEd Order No. 13 s. 2016.
observations.reason: Some reallocations and transfers were made to comply with prior recommendations; however, some SEF charges still need review and corrections; certain amounts were budgeted before issuance of AO and were re-aligned later.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer and Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to:

a. prioritize the preparation of Safe Closure and Rehabilitation Plan (SCRP) for the existing open disposal facility and submit to the Regional Office of the Environmental Management Bureau for their review and approval and implement once Authority to Close was issued; and

b. coordinate with the Regional Office of the Environmental Management Bureau the status of their 10-Year Solid Waste Management Plan and ensure that the same be approved by the National Solid Waste Management Commission within the year; and

c. require all barangay officials to establish fully operational Materials Recovery Facility (MRF) in every barangay or cluster of barangays pursuant to Section 32 of RA 9003.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality prepared the Safe Closure and Rehabilitation Plan and submitted to the Ecological Solid Waste Management Division on July 27, 2017. Rehabilitation was bided. The Municipality prepared the 10-Year Solid Waste Management Plan but it was still under review by NSWM. They conducted meetings with barangay officials; however, only one barangay had a fully operational MRF as of report.
observations.reason: Safe Closure Plan prepared and submitted but NSWM review pending; not all barangays have operational MRFs.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal LDRRMO and management prepared and submitted the LDRRM Plan. The unexpended balances were transferred to Trust Fund books in 2017. The Municipal Accountant transferred and disclosed the unexpended balances. The monthly Reports on Sources and Utilization were prepared but not all submitted within the prescribed period.
observations.reason: Some actions were completed (plan prepared, fund transfers done, disclosures made) but monthly reporting was not done within required timeframe.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Vice-Mayor, Sangguniang Bayan Members and the Municipal Budget Officer strictly observe the provisions of RA 7160 and National Budget Circular No. 563 in the appropriation relative to payment to various leagues and ensure that payments of membership fees and annual dues to private organizations shall not be charged against the government funds. We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant require the elected officials to submit the prescribed official receipt of the Republic of the Philippines in relation to the payment of their annual dues, membership fees and other fees paid to their respective leagues.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality stopped the payment of annual dues and membership fees to organizations not listed under RA 7160. The Municipal Accountant indicated they will require official receipts for validation in future disbursements.
observations.reason: While payments to non-allowable organizations were stopped, the Municipality still needs to ensure use of official Republic of the Philippines receipts in all such transactions and update supporting documentation procedures.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to address the causes for the delays in the remittance of the barangay shares from Real Property Tax (RPT) collection to enable the barangays to promptly meet the needs of their respective constituents.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality immediately remitted the shares of RPT to their respective barangays.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit all Disbursement Vouchers for the cash advance of Confidential and Intelligence Fund, with complete required supporting documents, to the concerned COA Audit Team within seven (7) working days from release of the check in accordance with the aforementioned rules and regulations. Henceforth, ensure that all officials and employees concerned strictly comply with the requirements of COA, DBM, DILG, GOCCs and DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted the required Disbursement Vouchers and supporting documents to the audit team and ensured compliance with COA, DBM, DILG and DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01. They indicated that they will adhere to the seven (7) working days submission requirement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Risk Reduction Management Officer submit the Report on Utilization of the Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 for the timely audit of the DRRM Fund.
observations.reason: No reason was provided for non-submission of the report monthly.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory
Committee  to  conduct  the  physical  count  of  all  Property,  Plant  and
Equipment at least once a year to ensure its existence, prepare  the  report
(RPCPPE) thereon with complete information  and  submit  to  the  Accounting
Office for reconciliation.

      We also recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  instruct  the  newly
appointed General Services Officer to prepare and  maintain  Property  Cards
in order to provide the balances per card  in  the  RPCPPE  and  immediately
identified the missing properties or discrepancies, if any, of the  PPEs  on
record against the actual count. Likewise, ensure that  the  Acknowledgement
Receipt of Equipment was renewed at least  every  three  years  or  whenever
there is a change in custodianship to established proper accountability.

      To ensure the accuracy, reliability and fair presentation of financial
statements, we further recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  oblige  the
Municipal Accountant to:


     a. Prepare the lapsing schedule with complete  information  consistent
        with the balances presented in the financial statements;


     b. Trace the  records  of  all  PPEs  and  provide  the  corresponding
        depreciation expenses; and

     c. Prepare and maintain the  required  Property  Plant  and  Equipment
        Ledger Card to facilitate the reconciliation  of  RPCPPE  with  the
        accounting records.

We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  instruct  the  Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal  Treasurer  to  ensure  that  their  respective
reports were reconciled so that Management will be properly guided in  their
decision making and avoid confusions to the users of their report.

      We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant  stop  the  practice
of reverting the unreleased checks to Cash and  Accounts  Payable  for  fair
presentation of the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:


     a. Local Finance Committee to adopt  strategy  in  order  to  increase
        revenue/collections and monitor properly the availability  of  cash
        every month to ensure that  all  income  due  the  Municipality  be
        collected in full and assure continuous flow of funds and that  the
        trust funds received be utilized only for the specific purpose  for
        which it was created; and


     b.  Municipal  Budget  Officer,  Municipal  Accountant  and  Municipal
        Treasurer to exercise diligence, prudence and proper control in the
        disposition of the  Municipality’s  funds  and  limit  expenditures
        within the total income realized and collected during the  year  to
        avoid incurrence of cash overdraft.

We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  instruct  the  Municipal
Accountant  and  the  Municipal  Treasurer  to  determine   the   cause   of
discrepancies  of  their  respective  reports  and  prepare  the   necessary
adjustments for a fair presentation  of  the  financial  statements.  Ensure
that the accounts of the agency shall be kept in such detail as  necessary
and observe consistency in keeping of accounts to  safeguard  against
inaccurate or misleading information pursuant to Section 111 of PD 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:


     a. Inventory Committee to  conduct  physical  count  of  all  supplies
        semestrally and prepare the corresponding Report of Physical  Count
        of Inventories and submit to  the  Accounting  Office  and  Auditor
        concerned within the prescribed period;


     b. Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer  to  prepare  and
        maintain the Supplies Ledger Cards and Stock Cards, respectively;


     c.  Municipal  Officials  concerned  to  implement   the   Requisition
        Procedures and the Reporting on Issuance of  Supplies/Materials  as
        required by the NGAS Manual for LGUs; and


     d. Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher to record  the
        appropriate supplies expense  accounts  based  on  the  Summary  of
        Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI).

We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  require  the   Municipal
Accountant to reconcile the municipal records with that  of  the  Provincial
Government of Batangas and settle the discrepancies between the balances  of
reciprocal accounts so that the Municipality will be  able  to  present  the
correct balances of inter-agency receivables and payables in  the  financial
statements.


      Likewise, the Municipal Mayor instruct  the  Municipal  Accountant  to
request from the Provincial Accountant the copies of JEVs  showing  all  the
transactions recorded in the books of accounts of the Province  that  should
be taken up in the Municipality’s book of accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:


     a. the  Municipal  Development  Council  to  ensure  that  the  Annual
        Investment Plan constitutes the LGUs’ priority  programs,  projects
        and activities (PPAs) to be integrated into the annual  budget  and
        avoid changes of plan that  will  affect  the  realization  of  the
        Municipality’s goals;


     b. the Municipal Development Council to clearly specify  the  detailed
        descriptions, locations and cost of priority programs, projects and
        activities funded out of 20 percent  Development  Fund  and  ensure
        that such projects partake the  nature  of  investment  or  capital
        expenditures. For projects that are presented in generic terms  and
        with lump-sum appropriation, secure the approval of the Sangguniang
        Bayan before the implementation of each project  and  disbursements
        of the fund; and


     c. the Municipal Budget Officer  and  Municipal  Accountant  to  avoid
        charging expenditures that do not contribute to the  attainment  of
        desirable  socio-economic  development  and  environmental  management
        outcomes  and  that  the  fund  be  utilized  only  for  the  projects
        identified and prioritized pursuant to the  established  rules  and
        regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal  Mayor  instruct  the  Local  School
Board, Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to:


     a. strictly observe the laws, rules and regulations and  that  SEF  be
        utilized exclusively for the purpose for which it  was  created  to
        promote the quality of education in every  public  schools  and  to
        enhance  the  delivery  of  service  to  all  constituents  in  the
        municipality. Avoid charging against the Special  Education  Fund
        the programs, projects and activities that can be funded out  of
        MOOE of Department of Education allocated to schools pursuant to
        Department of Education Order No. 13 s. 2016.  The amount of P471,623.08  will
        not be allowed in audit since it clearly violates Section 272 of RA
        7160 and Joint Circular issued by the DBM, DECS and DILG; and


     b. stop the practice of  allocating  the  five  percent  of  the  Special
        Education Fund budget for LDRRMF which is not  in  accordance  with
        Sections 100(c) and 272 of RA 7160.


      We also recommended that the Municipal  Mayor  require  the  Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to transfer  the  corresponding  cash
balances amounting P307,850.00 in the Special  Education  Fund  representing
the unexpended  balance  of  LDRRMF  allocated  from  SEF  and  prepare  the
necessary adjusting entries amounting to P25,000.00 for a fair  presentation
of the financial statements.

We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  require  the   Municipal
Environment  and  Natural  Resources  Officer  and  Municipal  Solid   Waste
Management Board to:


     a. prioritize the preparation of Safe  Closure  and  Rehabilitation  Plan
        (SCRP) for the existing open  disposal  facility  and  submit  to  the
        Regional Office of the Environmental Management  Bureau  for  their
        review and approval and be implemented once the Authority to  Close
        was issued; and


     b. coordinate with the Regional Office of the Environmental Management
        Bureau the status of their 10-Year Solid Waste Management Plan  and
        ensure that the  same  be  approve  by  the  National  Solid  Waste
        Management Commission within the year.


      Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor to require  all  the
barangay officials to  establish  a  fully  operational  Materials  Recovery
Facility in every barangay or cluster of barangays pursuant  to  Section  32
of RA 9003.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:


     a. Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management  Officer  (LDRRMO)  to
        prepare and submit  to  the  local  sanggunian  through  the  Local
        Disaster  Risk  Reduction  and   Management   Council   and   Local
        Development Council the annual LDRRM Plan and budget specifying  in
        detail the list of programs, activities and projects which  include
        under a separate caption those projects and programs charged to the
        unexpended LDRRMF of previous years, and implement at the  intended
        period of its implementation to ensure that the purpose  for  which
        the fund was established are met;


     b. Municipal Treasurer to  transfer  the  corresponding  cash  of  the
        unexpended balance of DRRMF-MOOE and 30%  QRF  in  the  Trust  Fund
        book;


     c. Municipal Accountant to disclose in  the  Notes  to  the  Financial
        Statements the amount and details  of  the  unexpended  balance  of
        LDRRMF in order to informed the users of relevant information; and


     d. LDRRMO to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF  on
        or before the 15th day after the end of each month through LDRRMC
        and Local Development Council (LDC) to the COA auditor pursuant  to
        COA Circular 2012-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Vice-Mayor, Sangguniang Bayan
Members and the Municipal Budget Officer strictly observe the provisions  of
RA 7160 and National Budget Circular No. 563 in the  appropriation  relative
to payment to various leagues and ensure that payments  of  membership  fees
and annual dues to private organization shall not  be  charged  against  the
government funds.


      We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor  instruct  the  Municipal
Treasurer and Municipal Accountant  to  require  the  elected  officials  to
submit the prescribed official receipt of the Republic  of  the  Philippines
in relation to the payment of their annual dues, membership fees  and  other
fees paid to their respective leagues considering that they  are  government
organization.

We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  instruct  the  Municipal
Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to address the causes for the  delays
in the remittance of the barangay shares from Real Property  Tax  (RPT)
collection to enable the barangays to  promptly  meet  the  needs  of  their
respective constituents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer
and other concerned officials to submit at the beginning  of  the  year  the
list of  all  on-going  government  projects/programs/activities  (PPA)  and
those that are to be implemented during the year,  in compliance  with  the
above cited regulations.

We recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  instruct  the  Municipal
Accountant to submit all Disbursement  Vouchers  for  the  cash  advance  of
Confidential  and  Intelligence  Fund,  with  complete  required  supporting
documents, to the concerned COA Audit Team within seven  (7)  working  days
from release of the check in accordance with the  aforementioned  rules  and
regulations. Henceforth, ensure that all officials and  employees  concerned
to strictly comply with the requirements of COA,  DBM,  DILG,  GOCCs  and  DND
Joint Circular No. 2015-01.

We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  direct   the   Municipal
Accountant to immediately  submit  the  required  reports  and  to  strictly
adhere with the rules on disclosing information in the  notes  to  financial
statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee that will conduct a complete and actual physical count of all LGU’s properties, prepare an inventory report thereon and submit the same to the Audit Team as required by the aforementioned regulation. We recommended that the General Services Office and Accounting Office regularly reconcile their records to determine the accuracy and correctness of the PPE accounts and present a more reliable financial statement. We recommended that the General Services Officer update the list of unserviceable properties and follow the procedures in Section 167-170 of COA Circular No. 92-386, for the immediate disposal to prevent further deterioration and loss of these properties.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor created the Inventory Committee per Administrative Order No. 10 s. 2016 dated August 1, 2016. The Municipality is in the process of updating and finalizing the list of unserviceable properties.
observations.reason: The Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) was not prepared due to lack of knowledge in accomplishing the said report. Lack of time and too much number of unserviceable properties.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require: the Municipal Accountant to recognize in the LGU books of accounts the assessed value of the 21 parcels of land for the meantime that it is not yet establish as to the mode these were acquired; the General Services Officer and Municipal Assessor to determine the nature of acquisition of these lands either through sale or donations to establish the basis on the valuation; the General Services Officer to conduct sites inspection and prepare reports on the actual usage of each property and existence of unlawful occupants, if any; and the Municipal Assessor to retrieve all the required information and documents necessary for the titling and refer the matter to the concerned agencies to facilitate the processing of titles.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality appropriated ₱96,000.00 for the titling of lands in CY 2016. The former General Services Officer conducted sites inspection. The Municipal Assessor applied and referred the four (4) lots to the Department of Environment and Natural Resources (DENR) to facilitate the processing of titles.
observations.reason: Lack of basis and supporting documents in booking up the 21 parcels of land. No reason was provided for non-preparation of report on the actual usage of each property. Insufficient funds for titling of lands.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer regularly prepare the Schedule/List of Delinquent Real Property Taxpayers before the deadline of submission of financial statements. Likewise, furnish the Municipal Accountant a copy of the said list for proper reconciliation and adjustments.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer prepared and submitted the List of Delinquent Real Property Taxpayers to the Office of the Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: Late submission by the Municipal Treasurer of the Certified List of Delinquent Taxpayers to the Accounting Office resulted in non-reconciliation of reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct: the Municipal Accountant to record the Notice of Disallowances amounting to P930,931.79 covered by the Notice of Finality of Decision and COA Order of Execution; and the Municipal Treasurer to deduct from the salary of persons liable as settlement of disallowances.
observations.managementAction: The amount of ₱930,931.79 was recorded in the book as Receivables-Disallowances/Charges per Journal Entry No. 2016-04-018 dated April 30, 2016. The Municipal Treasurer deducted from the salary of persons liable the disallowed amount on an installment basis.
observations.reason: Some of the persons liable were not connected to the Municipality the time the Notice of Finality of Decision and COA Order of Execution were received by the Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Honorable Municipal Mayor: Enjoin all concerned officials and employees to liquidate their cash advances within the prescribed period or immediately after the purposes have been served; Refrain from granting of additional cash advance to any official or employee unless the previous cash advance is first settled; Limit the granting of special cash advances to expenditures in which it is impractical to pay the same by check; Monitor regularly the cash advances to ensure adherence to the provisions of COA Circular 97-002 and withhold the salary of the concerned officials/employees who failed to settle their cash advances; Inform or remind the concerned officials and employees for the possible consequences of failure to liquidate the cash advances outstanding as of December 31, 2011.
observations.managementAction: The municipal officials and employees were reminded on the prescribed period of liquidation of their cash advances. The Municipal Accountant started to refrain on granting cash advances once his office notices that the concerned employees had existing cash advances. The Municipality limits the granting of cash advances as recommended. Verbal reminder was made by the Accounting Office to the concerned officials and employees.
observations.reason: Some cash advances were not liquidated on time due to failure on the part of some officials and employees to refund the excess amount of cash advances or provide its corresponding official receipt within the prescribed period. Granting of cash advances was not properly monitored. There were instances that they overlooked the existing unsettled cash advances of the concerned employees before granting new cash advances. During emergency cases cash advances were granted instead of direct payment to supplier.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the refund of ₱7,000.00 each official and employee for the excess in the ₱3,000.00 each allowable amount provided in Local Budget Circular No. 65 dated March 3, 1997. Refrain from paying personnel benefits not in accordance with rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality already deducted the disallowed amount to the employees who resigned/retired from their services. The Municipal Accountant ensures that all claims were supported with the required documents before processing for payments. The Municipal Accountant started to refrain granting cash advances once his office notices that the concerned employees had existing cash advances. The Office of the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Administrator ensures that all documents were complete before signing the contract agreement. The Application for Leave and Request for Monetization duly approved by the Municipal Mayor was already submitted to the Audit Team on June 9, 2016. The certified photocopy of Leave Cards was already submitted to the Audit Team on June 9, 2016. The Office of the Municipal Accountant always checks the completeness of the supporting documents before processing for payments.
observations.reason: The officials and employees still connected to the Municipality had a plan to make a request that the deductions be made on an installment basis.

observations.recommendation: We advised that the Municipal Accountant ensures that all claims were supported with the required documents before processing for payments. Ensure that all claims against government funds be supported with all the required documentations. Likewise, submit the lacking documents and refrain from paying disbursement vouchers with incomplete supporting documents to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office ensures that all claims were supported with the required documents before processing for payments.
observations.reason: No reason was provided by the Management for the non-submission of the noted lacking documents to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that maintenance of solid waste management site be appropriated from the General Fund Proper since these can be considered as regular expenses of the Municipality. We recommended that only capital expenditures enumerated in Section 3 of the Department of the Interior and Local Government and Department of Budget and Management Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 and included in the approved Annual Investment Plan (AIP) be charged against 20% Development Fund. Submit legal basis for charging the said expenses against 20% Development Fund.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer charged the regular maintenance of solid waste management site from the General Fund Proper and avoided charging against 20 percent Development Fund.
observations.reason: No reason was provided by the Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that each end-user units shall prepare their respective Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) and submit the same to the Budget Office for review and consolidation into the proposed Annual Procurement Plan (APP) and Annual Budget. Likewise, includes the procurement for infrastructure projects into the PPMP and APP and ensure that no procurement shall be undertaken unless it is in accordance with the approved APP.
observations.managementAction: All department/offices prepared their Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) for CY 2016 and were submitted to the Budget Office for review and subsequently forwarded to BAC Secretariat for consolidation into Annual Procurement Plan. Procurement for infrastructure projects was already included in the APP of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Infrastructure projects included in the Supplemental Budget were not included in the approved APP, however, no reason was provided for non-inclusion.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC Chairman advise the BAC Secretariat to post all Notices and Awards and Notices to Proceed, together with the approved contracts for the procurement of infrastructure projects, goods and services in the PhilGEPS, agency website within the prescribed period, as required under Sections 37.1.6 and 37.4.2 of the IRR of RA 9184. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor before signing the contract must see to it that all documents enumerated therein are complete. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor see to it that the time/duration and all the necessary conditions be indicated/stipulated in all contract agreements and in other bidding documents to determine the possible imposition of liquidated damages in case of delay.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Secretariat posted the Notice of Awards and Notices to Proceed, together with the approved contracts for the procurement of infrastructure projects, goods and services in the PhilGEPS in CY 2016. The Office of the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Administrator ensures that all documents were complete before signing the contract agreement. The Office of the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Administrator ensures that the time/duration and all necessary conditions were indicated/stipulated in all contract agreements and bidding documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require: the Municipal Budget Officer to submit an Application for Leave and Request for Monetization duly approved by the Municipal Mayor in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-001 and to refrain from appropriating in the annual budget the monetization of leave instead this should be charged to savings only as prescribed in LBC No. 2002-1. The Personnel Officer to submit the duly certified copy of the Employees Leave Card for this claim to determine the accuracy and correctness of the leave credits. The Municipal Accountant to refrain from paying claims with incomplete documentation beyond the limits imposed by any rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Application for Leave and Request for Monetization duly approved by the Municipal Mayor was already submitted to the Audit Team on June 9, 2016. The certified photocopy of Leave Cards was already submitted to the Audit Team on June 9, 2016. The Office of the Municipal Accountant always checks the completeness of the supporting documents before processing for payments.
observations.reason: Some claims were paid without the required documents, however, no reason was provided by the Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to closely monitor the take home pay of employees availing themselves of additional loans to ensure that they will not go beyond the required ₱4,000.00 minimum ceiling to comply with the provision of Section 45 of General Appropriations Act for Fiscal Year 2015. Also, the deductions that should be accommodated in the payroll are authorized and conformed with the General Appropriations Act applicable for the year.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor directed the Municipal Accountant to monitor the net take home pay of all employees in accordance with the provisions in General Appropriations Act for the year. The net take home pay of Municipal officials and employees starting July 2016 was in accordance with Section 52 of GAA for CY 2016 which is no lower than ₱3,500.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned municipal officials to consider the immediate termination of the time deposit and negotiate with the Bank Officials for the possible settlement and be used as payment for the loan availed with the Development Bank of the Philippines, Batangas City Branch, if applicable. We also recommended that in availing loan with banks to be used for the implementation of projects by the municipality the term of the elected officials be considered so as not to hamper the continuous development of the project which was financed out of the loan availed. In similar and future transactions, seek authority from the Sangguniang Bayan and approval from the Local Chief Executive before placing the idle funds in time deposit.
observations.managementAction: No additional loan was availed by the Municipality in CY 2016. No similar transaction occurred in CY 2016. The Municipality in the future will seek authority from the Sangguniang Bayan and approval from the Local Chief Executive before placing the idle funds in time deposit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to return the PDAF balances to the National Treasury for reversion to the unappropriated surplus in the General Fund of the national government.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality remitted/returned to the Bureau of Treasury the total amount of ₱124,076.01 per check no. 448485 dated June 28, 2016 and Oncoll Payment Slip reference no. 3402280440 dated July 12, 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned municipal official to refund the amount of ₱3,905.09 for the interest paid to BIR due to negligence of not remitting the taxes withheld within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer paid the amount of ₱3,905.09 per O.R. No. 18779351 dated July 21, 2016.
